key_predictive_features: BTC price (dominant driver, 0.89 correlation in 2025, BUT decoupling accelerating in 2026)
DeFi TVL on Ethereum (68% of global DeFi, growth signals expanding usage)
Gas fees / EIP-1559 burn rate (high activity = deflationary pressure = bullish)
Staking ratio (~30% of supply staked, reducing circulating supply)
Exchange reserves (multi-year lows = structural supply pressure)
DEX volume on Ethereum (record $139.6B Aug 2025 created price-decoupling from BTC)
ETH ETF inflows/outflows (institutional flow dominates short-term moves)
Active address growth rate (network adoption metric)
LSTM trend factor: RSI + gas usage with 20-day window (academic ML model)
K-Means clustered market state: MACD + active_address_growth + X_sentiment
fundamental_drivers: Ethereum ecosystem shift in 2026: L2s capturing more activity but base layer still settlement
~30% supply staked at 2.8-3.5% APY, creating structural supply lock
EIP-1559 burn mechanism: net deflationary during high-activity periods
Spot ETF inflows driving institutional adoption (multi-billion cumulative)
Competition from Solana/alternative L1s eroding some DeFi share
Protocol revenue declining as L2s capture fees (bearish for ETH value accrual)

cross_asset_correlations.BTC-USD: 0.89 declining toward 0.3-0.5 during DEX volume spikes -- 'Great Decoupling' thesis
cross_asset_correlations.DeFi_TVL: strong positive (ETH = infrastructure for DeFi growth)

seasonal_patterns: January effect: crypto generally higher returns late year into Jan
H1 outperformance vs USD and BTC historically, H2 underperformance
December: 80% win rate over last 5 years
Intraday: three activity phases aligned with Asian/European/US sessions
Monday nights (23:00-00:00) and Thursday mornings (00:00-01:00) show highest average returns
No strong day-of-week effect per academic neural network analysis (weak at best)
papers_and_models: ACM 2025: ML-driven multi-factor model for Ethereum -- LSTM trend + K-Means market state + autoencoder factor updates
Springer 2025: Neural network trading strategy for crypto with 400+ features and parametric labeling
tandfonline 2026: Bitcoin ETFs and structural decoupling -- ETH correlation with BTC dropping
Wiley 2025: ARIMA + deep learning for ETH price prediction with seasonal decomposition
Medium 2026: 'Great Decoupling' analysis -- BTC as macro asset, ETH as tech platform
recommended_new_signals: ETH staking ratio change signal: increasing stake = supply reduction = bullish
DeFi TVL velocity signal: rapid TVL growth on ETH = demand increase
Gas fee burn rate signal: high burn = deflationary = bullish (already have crypto_macro but it's 49.5%)
ETH-BTC correlation breakdown detector: when correlation drops below 0.5, trade ETH independently
ETH exchange reserve drawdown signal: declining reserves = accumulation = bullish
ETH ETF flow signal: net inflows > threshold = institutional buying
L2 activity spillover: high L2 usage still needs ETH for gas/settlement
deep_dives.implementation_notes: ETH at 49.5% 1d is our worst crypto performer. Root cause: our signals are BTC-derived and ETH is decoupling. The crypto_macro signal (DeFi TVL, staking, protocol revenue) should be ETH's best signal but it's at 49.5% too. Fix: (1) Add ETH-specific on-chain features (staking ratio, burn rate, exchange reserves) as a dedicated ETH signal, (2) When BTC-ETH correlation drops below 0.5, downweight BTC-correlated signals for ETH, (3) DEX volume spikes as regime-change detector. The academic ML model (LSTM with gas+RSI) is promising but requires training infrastructure we don't have yet.

deep_dives.current_accuracy: 46.4% 1d, 45.5% 3h (2450 samples) -- actively harmful, worse than random
key_predictive_features: BTC price (dominant, ~0.97 correlation, but leveraged 1.5-5x beta depending on regime)
mNAV premium/discount (current ~1.16x, range 0.97x to 2.8x -- KEY regime variable)
BTC ETF inflows/outflows (compete with MSTR for institutional BTC exposure)
Convertible bond structure ($8.25B convertible + $10.3B preferred ahead of common)
ATM equity issuance announcements (dilutive, creates sell-the-news pattern)
Short interest and options skew (put/call ratio 0.91x, avg 160d expiry)
10Y Treasury yield (negative for leveraged growth plays)
MSTR options implied vol vs BTC realized vol divergence
fundamental_drivers: BTC treasury: ~843K BTC at avg ~$66K cost basis
Software revenue negligible (~$100M/yr vs $56B BTC holdings)
Saylor's capital accretion strategy: issue equity at premium, buy BTC, increase BTC/share
mNAV premium collapsed from 2.8x (late 2024) to ~1.16x -- premium compression cycle
Capital structure: convertible debt + preferred equity senior to common = amplified downside
Asymmetric leverage: BTC -36% -> MSTR -44% in Jan-Feb 2026 (1.2x downside beta)
Halving cycle thesis weakening -- Saylor argues cycle is dead, price driven by capital flows

recommended_new_signals: mNAV premium/discount signal: BUY when mNAV < 1.0 (discount), SELL when mNAV > 2.0 (extreme premium)
Adaptive rolling 30d beta signal: replace static btc_proxy with time-varying beta
MSTR options IV vs BTC realized vol divergence: when MSTR IV >> BTC RV, expect mean reversion

deep_dives.implementation_notes: MSTR at 46.4% is our WORST ticker and actively losing money. The btc_proxy signal (46.5%) is the primary culprit -- it assumes static BTC correlation but MSTR has asymmetric leveraged beta that expands in stress. IMMEDIATE fixes: (1) Replace btc_proxy with rolling 30d OLS beta that adjusts for regime, (2) Add mNAV premium signal -- this is the single most important MSTR-specific feature and we don't have it, (3) Gate MSTR signals during high-vol regimes (the 4-state regime model would help), (4) Consider reducing MSTR signal count requirement or increasing confidence threshold given persistent underperformance. The Jan-Feb 2026 episode (BTC -36%, MSTR -44%) shows that downstream leverage risk is real.

key_predictive_features: Gold price and gold-silver ratio (mean-reverting, buy ratio>80, sell ratio<60, currently ~50)
Industrial demand: solar installations, EV production, AI data centers (59% of total demand)
COT positioning: speculative vs commercial net positions (CFTC weekly)
Physical silver ETF flows: SLV, PSLV inflows/outflows
DXY (strong inverse, -0.6 to -0.8)
Real interest rates / TIPS yield (inverse, -0.5 to -0.7)
Copper price (0.5-0.7 correlation, co-produced metal, industrial demand proxy)
Oil price (0.3-0.5, mining cost input and inflation proxy)
Supply deficit: 5th consecutive year of shortage in 2025, projected 46.3M oz deficit 2026
COMEX delivery month dynamics (Mar, May, Jul, Sep, Dec)
fundamental_drivers: Supply deficit persistent: mine output stagnating, 75% is byproduct of copper/lead/zinc mining
Industrial demand hit 700M oz in 2025 for first time (solar + AI + EVs)
Gold-silver ratio compressed from 105 to ~50 -- major mean reversion played out
Central bank gold buying creating spillover demand into silver
Green energy transition: silver critical for solar panels, structural multi-year demand growth
Price rose 147% in 2025, now at ~$114/oz -- potential exhaustion risk but deficit supports floor
Goldman target $85-100 (stale?), Citi target $110 H2 2026

Supply-demand structural analysis: 46.3M oz deficit projected 2026
recommended_new_signals: Gold-silver ratio mean reversion signal: BUY when ratio >80, SELL when ratio <60 (currently ~50 -- neutral)
Physical silver ETF flow signal: net SLV/PSLV inflows as accumulation indicator

Solar/EV production leading indicator: solar installation growth rate as 3-6 month demand predictor

Supply deficit persistence signal: annual deficit data as structural support level
Copper-silver divergence signal: if copper rallies but silver lags, expect silver catchup (co-production link)
deep_dives.implementation_notes: XAG 1d accuracy (49.3%) is poor but 3d (56.7%) and 5d (58.2%) are our best numbers across all tickers. This confirms silver is better predicted at longer horizons -- our 1d-focused system is the wrong timeframe for XAG. IMMEDIATE actions: (1) Shift XAG signal horizon to 3d/5d for position decisions, keep 1d only for intraday timing, (2) The gold-silver ratio at ~50 means the big mean-reversion trade has already played out -- don't chase it, (3) Focus cross-asset signals on DXY and copper as leading indicators, (4) Our metals_cross_asset signal (copper/GVZ/SPY/gold-silver ratio) is at 54.6% which is OK but could be improved by weighting DXY higher, (5) Seasonal window: we're in the weak May-Sep period -- reduce silver position sizing until the strong Dec-Feb window. 74 days without a metals trade remains concerning.
